name: WP Site Connector
description: Connect WordPress sites by URL and Application Password and read their posts, pages, media, and health.

tools.name: skeleton_refresh_sites_overview
tools.description: How many WordPress sites are connected.

tools.name: connect_site
tools.description: Connect a WordPress site by URL, username, and Application Password.

url.description: Full https:// URL of the WordPress site, e.g. https://example.com
url.title: Url

username.description: WordPress username that created the Application Password
username.title: Username

app_password.description: WordPress Application Password (from Users → Profile → Application Passwords)
app_password.title: App Password

tools.name: connect_site_ssh
tools.description: Connect a WordPress site over SSH ONLY, without a WordPress Application Password. Requires SSH access and WP-CLI on the server. Publishing/reading content for this site will then run entirely through WP-CLI over SSH instead of the REST API.

ssh_host.description: SSH hostname or IP address of the server
ssh_host.title: Ssh Host

wp_path.description: Absolute path to the WordPress installation on the server, e.g. /var/www/html
wp_path.title: Wp Path

ssh_key.description: SSH private key in PEM format. Use this OR ssh_password.
ssh_key.title: Ssh Key

ssh_password.description: SSH password. Use this OR ssh_key.
ssh_password.title: Ssh Password

tools.name: forget_site
tools.description: Disconnect a WordPress site and delete its stored credential.

site_id.description: Site id from a previous list_sites call — never invent it
site_id.title: Site Id

tools.name: add_ssh
tools.description: Add SSH access to a connected WordPress site to enable WP-CLI features: PHP version, plugin/theme/core update counts, cron jobs, database size.

site_id.description: Site id — set automatically by the panel form
site_id.title: Site Id

ssh_host.description: SSH hostname or IP address of the server
ssh_host.title: Ssh Host

wp_path.description: Absolute path to the WordPress installation on the server, e.g. /var/www/html
wp_path.title: Wp Path

ssh_key.description: SSH private key in PEM format. Use this OR ssh_password.
ssh_key.title: Ssh Key

ssh_password.description: SSH password. Use this OR ssh_key.
ssh_password.title: Ssh Password

tools.name: remove_ssh
tools.description: Remove SSH access from a connected WordPress site.

site_id.description: Site id from a previous list_sites call — never invent it
site_id.title: Site Id

tools.name: list_sites
tools.description: List the WordPress sites the user has connected.

tools.name: list_posts
tools.description: List recent posts on a connected WordPress site.

site_id.description: Site id from a previous list_sites call — never invent it
site_id.title: Site Id

tools.name: list_pages
tools.description: List pages on a connected WordPress site.

site_id.description: Site id from a previous list_sites call — never invent it
site_id.title: Site Id

tools.name: list_media
tools.description: List media library items on a connected WordPress site.

site_id.description: Site id from a previous list_sites call — never invent it
site_id.title: Site Id

tools.name: get_site_health
tools.description: Report read-only health for a connected WordPress site.

site_id.description: Site id from a previous list_sites call — never invent it
site_id.title: Site Id

tools.name: refresh_site
tools.description: Re-check connectivity and auth for a connected WordPress site and update its stored status.

site_id.description: Site id from a previous list_sites call — never invent it
site_id.title: Site Id

tools.name: refresh_all_sites
tools.description: Re-check connectivity for all connected WordPress sites at once.

tools.name: list_comments
tools.description: List comments on a connected WordPress site. Use status='hold' to see comments pending moderation, 'approved' for published, 'spam' for spam.

site_id.description: Site id from a previous list_sites call — never invent it
site_id.title: Site Id

tools.name: list_scheduled
tools.description: List posts scheduled for future publication on a connected WordPress site.

site_id.description: Site id from a previous list_sites call — never invent it
site_id.title: Site Id

tools.name: list_users
tools.description: List recently registered users on a connected WordPress site.

site_id.description: Site id from a previous list_sites call — never invent it
site_id.title: Site Id

tools.name: list_orders
tools.description: List WooCommerce orders on a connected WordPress site. Returns an error if WooCommerce is not installed.

site_id.description: Site id from a previous list_sites call — never invent it
site_id.title: Site Id

tools.name: list_custom_posts
tools.description: List items of a custom post type on a connected WordPress site. Use post_type= with the REST base slug (e.g. 'products', 'events', 'portfolio'). Check the site's panel to see available post types.

site_id.description: Site id from a previous list_sites call — never invent it
site_id.title: Site Id

post_type.description: REST base slug of the custom post type, e.g. 'products', 'events', 'portfolio'
post_type.title: Post Type

tools.name: get_server_info
tools.description: Get server information for a WordPress site via SSH + WP-CLI: PHP version, WordPress version, available plugin/theme/core updates, cron job count, database size. SSH must be configured first with add_ssh.

site_id.description: Site id from a previous list_sites call — never invent it
site_id.title: Site Id

tools.name: create_post
tools.description: Create a new post on a connected WordPress site. Defaults to status='draft' — pass status='publish' to publish immediately, or status='future' with a date to schedule.

site_id.description: Site id from a previous list_sites call — never invent it
site_id.title: Site Id

content.description: Post body as HTML. To place an image INSIDE the text at a specific spot, embed an tag (or a block) at that exact position — use the url returned by a prior upload_media call. WordPress renders the content top-to-bottom exactly as given.
content.title: Content

date.description: ISO 8601 datetime for scheduled publication — required when status='future'
date.title: Date

slug.description: Custom URL slug (e.g. 'infinityfree-review-2026' to publish at https://site.com/infinityfree-review-2026/). Omit to let WordPress auto-generate one from the title.
slug.title: Slug

featured_media_id.description: Media library id to use as the featured image (cover) — get this from a prior upload_media call. This sets the post's cover, separate from any images embedded in content.
featured_media_id.title: Featured Media Id

tools.name: update_post
tools.description: Update an existing post on a connected WordPress site — title, content, status, excerpt, or scheduled date. Only fields you pass are changed.

site_id.description: Site id from a previous list_sites call — never invent it
site_id.title: Site Id

post_id.description: WordPress post ID from a previous list_posts call — never invent it
post_id.title: Post Id

title.description: New title — omit to leave unchanged
title.title: Title

content.description: New body as HTML — omit to leave unchanged. To place an image INSIDE the text, embed at that exact position using a url from a prior upload_media call.
content.title: Content

status.description: New status: 'draft', 'publish', 'pending', 'future' — omit to leave unchanged
status.title: Status

excerpt.description: New excerpt — omit to leave unchanged
excerpt.title: Excerpt

date.description: New ISO 8601 scheduled datetime — omit to leave unchanged
date.title: Date

slug.description: New custom URL slug — omit to leave unchanged
slug.title: Slug

featured_media_id.description: New media library id for the featured image (cover) — omit to leave unchanged. Get this from a prior upload_media call.
featured_media_id.title: Featured Media Id

tools.name: upload_media
tools.description: Upload an image to a connected WordPress site's Media Library. Returns a media_id (use as featured_media_id on create_post/update_post for the cover image) and a url (embed as inside a post's content to place it anywhere in the text).

site_id.description: Site id from a previous list_sites call — never invent it
site_id.title: Site Id

files.description: FileUpload payload (list[dict] with data_base64/name/content_type) — the image file to upload to the WordPress Media Library.
files.title: Files

title.description: Optional media title — defaults to the filename
title.title: Title

secrets.name: wp_encryption_key
secrets.description: Fernet key used to encrypt WordPress Application Passwords and SSH credentials before they reach storage. Shared across all users; generate once with `Fernet.generate_key()` and set in Developer Portal → Secrets.
